Remboursements et dégrèvements d’impôts.d: Covers mandatory refunds, tax credits, and abatements on State taxes (income tax, corporate tax, VAT). This is an evaluative-credit programme that adjusts automatically based on taxpayer claims and legislative entitlements.
Remboursements et dégrèvements d’impôts.b: Approximately 38-40 million tax households and all VAT-registered businesses in France. The programme does not ’serve’ beneficiaries in a social sense; it processes fiscal corrections owed by the State.

Avances sur le montant des impositions r.d: Provides monthly cash advances to local authorities (communes, departments, regions, EPCI) on the proceeds of local taxes collected by the State on their behalf, before final settlement. This ensures continuous funding of local government operations.
Avances sur le montant des impositions r.b: Approximately 35,000 communes, 101 departments, 18 regions, and thousands of EPCIs and other local bodies across metropolitan and overseas France.

Pensions civiles et militaires de retrai.d: Finances retirement pensions for civil servants (État) and military personnel. Funded by employer contributions from ministries and employee payroll deductions, managed as a special-purpose account (CAS).
Pensions civiles et militaires de retrai.b: Approximately 2.6 million retirees and their surviving beneficiaries (about 1.6 million former civil servants and 1 million military pensioners and reversionary rights holders).

Charge de la dette et trésorerie de l’Ét.d: Covers interest payments on government debt (OATs, BTF, inflation-indexed bonds) and cash management costs. Managed by the Agence France Trésor (AFT) under the Ministry of Finance.
Charge de la dette et trésorerie de l’Ét.b: Not applicable (no direct individual beneficiaries). Bondholders are institutional investors, foreign central banks, insurance companies, and retail investors holding French sovereign debt.

Enseignement scolaire public du second d.d: Funds public secondary education (collèges and lycées), including teacher salaries, pedagogical support staff, and educational operations for grades 6 through terminale across metropolitan and overseas France.
Enseignement scolaire public du second d.b: Approximately 5.6 million students enrolled in public secondary schools (about 3.4 million in collèges and 2.2 million in lycées généraux, technologiques et professionnels).

Enseignement scolaire public du premier.d: Funds public primary education (écoles maternelles and élémentaires), covering teacher salaries, training, and pedagogical supervision for children aged 3 to 11.
Enseignement scolaire public du premier.b: Approximately 5.7 million students in public primary schools (about 2.5 million in maternelle and 3.2 million in élémentaire), plus approximately 350,000 teachers.

Soutien de la politique de la défense.d: Funds transversal support functions for the Ministry of Armed Forces: human resources, infrastructure, information systems, real estate management, and general administration. It is the ministry’s backbone programme.
Soutien de la politique de la défense.b: Approximately 270,000 military and civilian personnel of the Ministry of Armed Forces benefit from the HR, infrastructure, and IT services funded by this programme.

Équipement des forces.d: Finances the acquisition, development, and maintenance of major military equipment systems: combat aircraft (Rafale), submarines (Barracuda), armoured vehicles (Scorpion/Griffon), satellites, and naval vessels.
Équipement des forces.b: Not applicable in the individual sense. Serves the three armed services (Armée de terre, Marine nationale, Armée de l’air et de l’espace) and the defence industrial base (Dassault, Naval Group, MBDA, Thales, etc.).

Aide à l’accès au logement.d: Funds personal housing allowances (APL, ALS, ALF) that subsidize rents and mortgage payments for low-income households, students, and families. Since 2021, benefits are calculated on real-time income (contemporaneous basis).
Aide à l’accès au logement.b: Approximately 6.5 million households (around 13 million individuals) receiving one of the three forms of housing aid, managed by CAF/MSA.

Préparation et emploi des forces.d: Funds the operational readiness, training, and deployment of French armed forces, including OPEX (external operations), domestic operations (Sentinelle), exercises, and day-to-day activity of military units.
Préparation et emploi des forces.b: Approximately 200,000 active military personnel across all services who are trained and deployed under this programme, plus forces engaged in external operations (Sahel, Indo-Pacific, etc.).

Accompagnement des mutations économiques.d: Funds employment support measures including subsidized contracts, apprenticeship incentives, professional training, business competitiveness clusters, and support for workers affected by economic restructuring.
Accompagnement des mutations économiques.b: Several million workers and jobseekers annually. Key sub-programmes: approximately 800,000 apprenticeship contracts, hundreds of thousands of subsidized employment contracts (PEC, CIE), and professional training beneficiaries.

Handicap et dépendance.d: Funds the Allocation aux adultes handicapés (AAH) and related disability benefits, support for disabled workers in ESAT establishments, and contributions to the care of dependent persons in specialised facilities.
Handicap et dépendance.b: Approximately 1.3 million AAH recipients, plus around 120,000 workers in ESAT (établissements et services d’aide par le travail), and beneficiaries of other disability-related aids.

Inclusion sociale et protection des pers.d: Funds the prime d’activité (in-work benefit), food aid programmes, protection of vulnerable adults (tutelle/curatelle), and social inclusion measures for the most disadvantaged populations.
Inclusion sociale et protection des pers.b: Approximately 4.6 million households receiving the prime d’activité (around 9-10 million individuals), plus beneficiaries of food aid (associations serving 5+ million people) and persons under legal protection (about 800,000).

Police nationale.d: Funds the operations, personnel, equipment, and infrastructure of the Police nationale, covering public safety, judicial police investigations, border control (PAF), intelligence (DGSI funded separately), and CRS riot police.
Police nationale.b: Not applicable as individual beneficiaries. The programme employs approximately 152,000 police officers and administrative staff, serving the entire population in police zones (urban areas, about 50% of territory covering ~70% of population).

Gendarmerie nationale.d: Funds the operations, personnel, equipment, and infrastructure of the Gendarmerie nationale, responsible for public safety, judicial police, and defence missions in rural areas and small towns (95% of national territory).
Gendarmerie nationale.b: Not applicable as individual beneficiaries. Employs approximately 100,000 military gendarmes and 4,000 civilian staff, responsible for security across 95% of French territory covering about 30% of the population.

Aide économique et financière au dévelop.d: Funds bilateral economic and financial development aid, primarily through the Agence française de développement (AFD), including concessional loans, grants to developing countries, contributions to multilateral development banks, and debt relief initiatives (C2D).
Aide économique et financière au dévelop.b: Indirect beneficiaries span populations of partner countries, primarily in Sub-Saharan Africa (19 priority countries designated by the CICID), Southeast Asia, and the Middle East. No single individual beneficiary count applicable.

Formations supérieures et recherche univ.d: Funds French public universities and grandes écoles under the Ministry of Higher Education: operating subsidies, faculty salaries, university research, student-facing infrastructure, and the Parcoursup admissions system.
Formations supérieures et recherche univ.b: Approximately 1.7 million students enrolled in public universities and attached institutions, plus about 90,000 teaching and research staff (enseignants-chercheurs) and 60,000 BIATSS support staff.

Recherches scientifiques et technologiqu.d: Funds France’s major public research organisations (organismes de recherche): CNRS, INSERM, INRIA, CEA (civil), IRD, and others. Covers researcher salaries, laboratory operations, large-scale scientific infrastructure, and ANR (Agence nationale de la recherche) grants.
Recherches scientifiques et technologiqu.b: Approximately 35,000 researchers and 40,000 support staff across EPST/EPIC bodies, plus thousands of PhD candidates and postdocs funded through ANR project grants. Indirectly serves the entire national research ecosystem.

Enseignement privé du premier et du seco.d: Funds teacher salaries and operating subsidies for private schools under contract (sous contrat) with the State, both at primary and secondary levels, primarily Catholic schools. The State pays teachers; local authorities contribute to operating costs.
Enseignement privé du premier et du seco.b: Approximately 2.1 million students in private schools under contract (about 17% of total school enrolment), and approximately 140,000 teachers paid by the State in these establishments.

Vie de l’élève.d: Funds student welfare services in primary and secondary schools: school health services (médecine scolaire), social workers, school scholarships (bourses), special needs assistants (AESH), and school climate initiatives including anti-bullying programmes.
Vie de l’élève.b: Approximately 3 million scholarship recipients (bourses de collège and lycée), around 130,000 AESH accompanying 430,000+ disabled students, plus all students benefiting from health and social services.

Accès et retour à l’emploi.d: Funds the public employment service (France Travail, ex-Pôle emploi), subsidized employment contracts for priority groups, local employment initiatives (missions locales for youth), and insertion through economic activity (IAE structures).
Accès et retour à l’emploi.b: France Travail registers approximately 5.5 million jobseekers (categories A-E). Missions locales serve about 1.1 million young people aged 16-25. IAE structures employ around 140,000 persons in insertion pathways annually.

Amortissement de la dette de l’État liée.d: Dedicated special account (CAS) for the progressive repayment of Covid-19-related government debt over 20 years (2022-2042). Funded by earmarked revenue from a fraction of corporate and income tax receipts.
Amortissement de la dette de l’État liée.b: Not applicable (debt amortisation programme). Indirectly benefits all French taxpayers by ring-fencing and scheduling the repayment of pandemic-era borrowing, estimated at 165 billion euros.

Hébergement, parcours vers le logement e.d: Funds emergency shelters (hébergement d’urgence), transitional housing (CHRS), the 115 emergency hotline, accommodation for asylum seekers, and ’Housing First’ (Un chez-soi d’abord) programmes for homeless and vulnerable populations.
Hébergement, parcours vers le logement e.b: Approximately 200,000+ emergency shelter places nightly (record levels since 2020), serving an estimated 300,000+ homeless persons annually, including families, isolated individuals, and persons in irregular situations.

Justice judiciaire.d: Funds the operations of civil and criminal courts (tribunaux judiciaires, cours d’appel, Cour de cassation), including magistrate and clerk salaries, legal aid (aide juridictionnelle), and court infrastructure modernisation.
Justice judiciaire.b: Approximately 9,000 magistrates and 24,000 greffiers/fonctionnaires serving the judicial system. Legal aid (aide juridictionnelle) benefits about 1 million litigants annually. The entire justiciable population (68 million residents) is indirectly served.

Administration pénitentiaire.d: Funds the operation of prisons (établissements pénitentiaires), probation and reintegration services (SPIP), prison construction/renovation programmes, and healthcare and vocational training for inmates.
Administration pénitentiaire.b: Approximately 74,000 inmates (as of 2024, chronic overcrowding at ~120% capacity in maisons d’arrêt), plus about 170,000 persons under probation/community supervision. Employs approximately 44,000 prison staff (surveillants, CPIP, administrative).

Urbanisme, territoires et amélioration d.d: Funds urban planning, housing renovation (through ANAH and MaPrimeRénov’ energy retrofit grants), social housing production support, and territorial planning operations including ORT (Opérations de revitalisation de territoire).
Urbanisme, territoires et amélioration d.b: MaPrimeRénov’ served approximately 670,000 households in 2023. ANAH overall assists about 800,000 households annually. Social housing production targets 100,000+ new HLM units per year. Territorial programmes affect thousands of communes.

Compétitivité et durabilité de l’agricul.d: Funds national agricultural support measures complementing the EU Common Agricultural Policy: CUMA investment grants, installation aid for young farmers, plant/animal health services, SAFER operations, forestry policy (ONF), and agricultural crisis response.
Compétitivité et durabilité de l’agricul.b: Approximately 390,000 farms in France, with particular focus on young farmer installation (about 14,000 annual installations) and small/medium farms in transition. ONF manages 11 million hectares of public forests.

Service public de l’énergie.d: Funds public service obligations in the energy sector: support for renewable energy producers (obligation d’achat), electricity price equalisation for overseas territories (péréquation tarifaire), energy poverty measures (chèque énergie), and Mediateur de l’énergie.
Service public de l’énergie.b: Chèque énergie reaches approximately 5.6 million low-income households. Overseas territories (2.2 million inhabitants) benefit from price equalisation. Thousands of renewable energy producers receive feed-in tariffs or complements de rémunération.

Concours financiers aux communes et grou.d: Channels State grants to communes and intercommunal bodies (EPCI): the Dotation globale de fonctionnement (DGF), Dotation d’équipement des territoires ruraux (DETR), Dotation de soutien à l’investissement local (DSIL), and related equalisation grants.
Concours financiers aux communes et grou.b: Approximately 35,000 communes and 1,250+ EPCIs receive DGF. DETR benefits about 33,000 eligible rural communes. DSIL supports investment in communes of all sizes. Total envelope approximately 28 Md€ including FCTVA across the broader mission.

Vie étudiante.d: Funds student grants (bourses sur critères sociaux), university student housing (CROUS residences), student restaurants (RU at subsidised prices), and student health services. Managed through the CNOUS/CROUS network.
Vie étudiante.b: Approximately 800,000 students receiving bourses sur critères sociaux, 175,000 CROUS housing places, and student restaurants serving approximately 300,000 meals daily across 700+ points of service.

Sécurité et qualité sanitaires de l’alim.d: Funds official food safety controls (DGAL inspections), animal health programmes (vaccination campaigns, epizootic response including avian influenza), phytosanitary inspections, and the food safety agency ANSES’s contribution.
Sécurité et qualité sanitaires de l’alim.b: Protects the entire French population (68 million) through food safety. Directly involves approximately 400,000 food establishments under inspection, livestock farms for animal health surveillance, and agricultural producers subject to phytosanitary controls.

France Télévisions.d: Funds the public television group France Télévisions (France 2, France 3, France 5, franceinfo, Outre-mer la 1ère, france.tv). Since 2023 suppression of the redevance, funded directly from the State budget to guarantee public service broadcasting independence.
France Télévisions.b: France Télévisions reaches approximately 50 million weekly viewers across its channels. Employs about 9,300 staff. Serves the entire French-speaking population including overseas territories through regional programming.

Patrimoines.d: Funds the preservation, restoration, and promotion of France’s cultural heritage: historic monuments (45,000+ protected sites), national museums (musées nationaux including Louvre, Orsay, Versailles), archives nationales, and archaeological research (INRAP).
Patrimoines.b: France’s 45,000+ monuments historiques (14,000 classés, 31,000 inscrits), approximately 1,200 musées de France, and millions of annual visitors. The Louvre alone receives about 9 million visitors yearly. Indirectly benefits all heritage property owners receiving restoration grants.

Création.d: Funds support for artistic creation across performing arts (theatre, dance, music, circus, street arts), visual arts, and cultural venues: national theatres, opera houses, orchestras, contemporary art centres (FRAC), and artist residency programmes.
Création.b: Supports approximately 350 labellised cultural institutions (CDN, Scènes nationales, SMAC, etc.), thousands of subsidised artistic companies and individual artists, and the national opera and theatre companies (Opéra de Paris, Comédie-Française, etc.).

Infrastructures et services de transport.d: Funds maintenance and development of State transport infrastructure: national road network (12,000 km), waterways (Voies navigables de France), port infrastructure, rail network contributions (alongside SNCF Réseau), and intermodal transport services.
Infrastructures et services de transport.b: Serves the entire French population through transport networks. Specifically: users of 12,000 km of national roads, 6,700 km of navigable waterways, 11 major maritime ports (grands ports maritimes), and railway infrastructure maintenance benefiting 4.7 million daily rail passengers.

Sport.d: Funds the development of sport in France: high-performance sport (INSEP, preparation for Olympic/Paralympic games), grassroots sport development through the Agence nationale du Sport, anti-doping agency (AFLD), and major sporting events hosting.
Sport.b: Approximately 16 million licensed athletes in 360,000 sports clubs across France. High-performance programme supports about 7,000 athletes on the ministerial lists. Paris 2024 preparations involved significant infrastructure investment.

Jeunesse et vie associative.d: Funds youth engagement programmes, primarily the Service civique (voluntary civic service for 16-25 year-olds), the Service national universel (SNU), FDVA grants to associations, youth information networks, and support for community organisations (vie associative).
Jeunesse et vie associative.b: Service civique engages approximately 150,000 young volunteers annually. SNU has involved several tens of thousands of participants since its experimental launch. FDVA supports thousands of local associations. Overall approximately 1.3 million associations in France benefit from the policy framework.

Prévention des risques.d: Funds prevention and management of technological, natural, and health-environmental risks: ICPE (classified installations) inspections, flood prevention plans (PAPI), contaminated site remediation, ADEME contributions, and air quality monitoring (AASQA networks).
Prévention des risques.b: Protects the entire population from environmental risks. Specifically: 500,000+ ICPE installations subject to inspection, 17 million residents in flood-prone areas, populations near Seveso sites (about 1,300 in France), and communities affected by former mining/industrial contamination.

Énergie, climat et après-mines.d: Funds energy transition measures (hydrogen strategy, decarbonisation of industry), climate change adaptation, and post-mining obligations (former coal basin environmental remediation in Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Lorraine, etc. via BRGM/DREAL).
Énergie, climat et après-mines.b: Post-mining measures serve populations in former coal and mining basins (several hundred thousand residents). Energy transition measures benefit industrial sectors and territorial climate plans. Hydrogen strategy supports emerging industrial ecosystem.

Sécurité civile.d: Funds national civil protection resources: aerial firefighting fleet (Canadair, Dash), bomb disposal units (déminage), FORMISC military emergency units, COGIC crisis coordination centre, and support to departmental fire services (SDIS).
Sécurité civile.b: Protects the entire French population (68 million). The aerial fleet fights about 3,000-4,000 wildfires annually. Approximately 250,000 sapeurs-pompiers (40,000 professional, 195,000 volunteer, 15,000 military in Paris/Marseille) form the national response system.

Immigration et asile.d: Funds the asylum system (OFPRA processing, CNDA appeals court, CMA reception centres), immigration enforcement (CRA detention centres, removal operations), integration programmes (CIR), and the OFII agency operations.
Immigration et asile.b: OFPRA processes approximately 140,000-160,000 asylum applications annually. National reception system (DNA) provides about 110,000 places in CADA/HUDA/CPH centres. CIR integration programme serves about 110,000 newly arrived legal immigrants per year.

Enseignement technique agricole.d: Funds public and private agricultural secondary and technical education (lycées agricoles, MFR), covering teacher salaries, operating costs, and farm/laboratory infrastructure in agricultural educational establishments.
Enseignement technique agricole.b: Approximately 190,000 students (about 60% in private establishments under contract, 40% in public lycées agricoles), with approximately 15,000 teachers. Includes 800+ establishments offering CAP to BTS-level qualifications in agriculture, agro-food, and environment.

Protection judiciaire de la jeunesse.d: Funds the judicial protection of minors: educational measures ordered by juvenile judges, youth detention centres (CEF), juvenile prisons (EPM), open-environment educational programmes (milieu ouvert), and the sector associatif habilité providing care services.
Protection judiciaire de la jeunesse.b: Approximately 150,000 minors followed by PJJ services annually (both penal and civil measures), with about 1,200 places in CEF and 800 in EPM/quartiers mineurs. The DPJJ employs about 9,000 staff across 220+ units.

Politique de la ville.d: Funds urban renewal and social cohesion programmes in designated priority neighbourhoods (QPV): ANRU urban renovation projects (NPNRU), cité éducative programmes, employment insertion, social mediation, and associations working in disadvantaged urban areas.
Politique de la ville.b: Approximately 5.4 million residents living in 1,514 QPV (quartiers prioritaires de la politique de la ville). NPNRU covers 450 neighbourhoods with major urban renewal projects (estimated 27 Md€ total investment over 10+ years including leveraged funds).

Coordination du travail gouvernemental.d: Funds the Prime Minister’s coordination services: SGDSN (defence/national security secretariat), ANSSI (cybersecurity agency), strategic intelligence coordination, government communication (SIG), independent administrative authorities (CNIL, CADA), and interministerial coordination bodies.
Coordination du travail gouvernemental.b: Not applicable as individual beneficiaries. ANSSI protects approximately 700 OIV (opérateurs d’importance vitale) and critical infrastructure. CNIL handles approximately 16,000 complaints annually. Services benefit the entire government apparatus and indirectly the whole population.

Conseil d’État et autres juridictions ad.d: Funds the administrative justice system: Conseil d’État (supreme administrative court and government legal advisor), 42 tribunaux administratifs, 9 cours administratives d’appel, and the Cour nationale du droit d’asile (CNDA) which handles asylum appeals.
Conseil d’État et autres juridictions ad.b: Administrative courts handle approximately 250,000 cases annually (including about 70,000 CNDA asylum appeals). Employs about 4,200 staff including approximately 1,300 magistrats administratifs. Serves all persons contesting administrative decisions.

Financement des investissements stratégi.d: Funds France 2030 strategic investments (54 Md€ total envelope): decarbonised hydrogen, electric vehicles/batteries, nuclear innovation (SMR), biotechnologies, space, semiconductors, quantum computing, key minerals, and cultural/creative industries. Managed by the SGPI.
Financement des investissements stratégi.b: Supports thousands of businesses, startups, and research laboratories across strategic sectors. Key targets: 8 gigafactories for batteries, 2 SMR nuclear reactors, 20 biomedical innovations, and a French semiconductor ecosystem. No single individual beneficiary count.

Emploi outre-mer.d: Funds employment support measures specific to overseas territories: employer social contribution exemptions (exonérations Lodeom), service militaire adapté (SMA) vocational training for youth, and insertion programmes tailored to DOM-TOM labour markets.
Emploi outre-mer.b: Approximately 100,000+ employers in overseas territories benefit from Lodeom contribution exemptions. SMA trains about 6,000 young volunteers annually (aged 18-25) across 8 overseas regiments. Total overseas population: 2.2 million.

Protection maladie.d: Funds the Aide médicale d’État (AME), which provides healthcare coverage to undocumented immigrants residing in France for more than 3 months and meeting income criteria. Also covers soins urgents for those not eligible for AME and healthcare for other specific populations.
Protection maladie.b: Approximately 400,000 AME beneficiaries (as of end 2023, rising trend). Soins urgents cover additional undocumented persons not meeting AME criteria. The programme has been politically contentious with periodic reform debates.

Solidarité à l’égard des pays en dévelop.d: Funds bilateral development cooperation managed by the Ministry of Europe and Foreign Affairs (MEAE): grants to developing countries for governance, health, education, and food security; contributions to multilateral organisations (Global Fund, GAVI, GPE); crisis humanitarian aid; Expertise France; and French development volunteers.
Solidarité à l’égard des pays en dévelop.b: Indirect beneficiaries across 19 priority partner countries (primarily Sahel, West Africa, Great Lakes). Key multilateral contributions: Global Fund against AIDS/TB/Malaria (serving millions), GAVI vaccination alliance, Global Partnership for Education. French development volunteers number about 2,000.

Environnement et prospective de la polit.d: Defense foresight, intelligence, strategic research, and international military relations.

Reconnaissance et réparation en faveur d.d: Pensions, benefits, and recognition programs for veterans, war disabled, and their dependents.
Reconnaissance et réparation en faveur d.b: Veterans, war disabled, war orphans, former combatants

Soutien de la politique de l’éducation n.d: Administrative support, HR management, IT systems, and evaluation functions for the national education ministry.
Soutien de la politique de l’éducation n.b: Ministry of Education central and deconcentrated services

Sécurité et éducation routières.d: Road safety policy, driving license management, awareness campaigns, and traffic regulation.

Administration territoriale de l’État.d: Prefectures, sub-prefectures, and deconcentrated state administration across departments and regions.

Conduite et pilotage des politiques de l.d: Central administration, legal affairs, IT, and cross-cutting support for the Ministry of the Interior.
Conduite et pilotage des politiques de l.b: Ministry of the Interior staff and services

Vie politique.d: Organization of elections, political party financing, and funding of political campaigns.

Accès au droit et à la justice.d: Legal aid, access to justice for disadvantaged populations, and victim support services.
Accès au droit et à la justice.b: Low-income litigants, crime victims, legal aid beneficiaries

Conduite et pilotage de la politique de.d: Central administration, IT systems, HR, and strategic support for the Ministry of Justice.
Conduite et pilotage de la politique de.b: Ministry of Justice staff and services

Conseil supérieur de la magistrature.d: Independent body overseeing magistrate appointments, discipline, and judicial ethics.

Développement des entreprises et régulat.d: Business support, competition regulation, consumer protection, and industrial policy implementation.

Stratégies économiques.d: Economic strategy, Treasury operations, international financial negotiations, and public finance steering.

Statistiques et études économiques.d: Production of official economic statistics and studies by INSEE and ministerial statistical services.
Statistiques et études économiques.b: INSEE, public and private decision-makers, researchers

Plan France Très haut débit.d: Rollout of ultra-fast broadband (fiber) across French territory, especially underserved areas.
Plan France Très haut débit.b: Households, businesses, local authorities in low-connectivity areas

Facilitation et sécurisation des échange.d: Customs operations, trade facilitation, border controls, and combating fraud and trafficking.

Conduite et pilotage des politiques écon.d: Central administration, HR, IT, and cross-cutting support for the economy and finance ministries.
Conduite et pilotage des politiques écon.b: Ministry of Economy and Finance staff

Action de la France en Europe et dans le.d: French diplomatic network, contributions to international organizations, and foreign policy implementation.

Diplomatie culturelle et d’influence.d: Cultural diplomacy, promotion of French language, academic cooperation, and the Alliance Française network.
Diplomatie culturelle et d’influence.b: Institut Français, Alliance Française, foreign students and cultural actors

Français à l’étranger et affaires consul.d: Consular services for French nationals abroad including civil status, elections, and social assistance.

Paysages eau et biodiversité.d: Protection of landscapes, water resources, and biodiversity including Natura 2000 and national parks.
Paysages eau et biodiversité.b: OFB, national parks, water agencies, protected areas

Expertise information géographique et mé.d: Funding for Météo-France and IGN for weather forecasting, climate services, and geographic information.

Énergie climat et après-mines.d: Energy transition policy, climate action, and post-mining site remediation and social guarantees.
Énergie climat et après-mines.b: Former mining communities, energy transition actors

Sûreté nucléaire et radioprotection.d: Nuclear safety regulation and radiation protection through the ASN and IRSN.
Sûreté nucléaire et radioprotection.b: ASN, IRSN, nuclear operators, general public

Affaires maritimes pêche et aquaculture.d: Maritime safety, fisheries management, aquaculture support, and seafarer training.
Affaires maritimes pêche et aquaculture.b: Fishermen, aquaculture operators, seafarers, coastal communities

Recherche spatiale.d: France’s contribution to ESA and CNES space research and exploration programs.

Recherche duale.d: Dual-use (civil-military) research funding notably for CEA and CNES programs.

Recherche dans les domaines de l’énergie.d: Energy research including nuclear, renewables, and energy efficiency through CEA and other agencies.

Enseignement supérieur et recherche agri.d: Agricultural higher education and research institutions including veterinary schools.
Enseignement supérieur et recherche agri.b: Agricultural schools, INRAE, veterinary students and researchers

Égalité entre les femmes et les hommes.d: Gender equality policy, combating violence against women, and professional equality promotion.

Accompagnement des mutations économiques.d: Vocational training, apprenticeship support, and skills adaptation to economic transitions.
Accompagnement des mutations économiques.b: Workers in transition, apprentices, training organizations

Amélioration de la qualité de l’emploi.d: Labor inspection, occupational health and safety, and improvement of working conditions.

Soutien des ministères sociaux.d: Administrative support, IT, HR, and cross-cutting functions for social affairs ministries.
Soutien des ministères sociaux.b: Social affairs ministry staff and services

Fonction publique.d: Cross-cutting civil service policy including training (ENA/INSP), social action, and interministerial HR.

Urbanisme territoires et amélioration de.d: Urban planning, housing construction incentives, ANAH renovation subsidies, and habitat improvement.
Urbanisme territoires et amélioration de.b: ANAH, homeowners, social housing operators, local authorities

Hébergement parcours vers le logement.d: Emergency shelter, transitional housing, and pathways to permanent housing for homeless persons.

Impulsion et coordination de la politiqu.d: Territorial planning coordination, ANCT support, and balanced regional development initiatives.
Impulsion et coordination de la politiqu.b: ANCT, rural and underserved territories, local authorities

Transmission des savoirs et démocratisat.d: Cultural education, arts training schools, and broadening public access to culture.
Transmission des savoirs et démocratisat.b: Cultural education students, general public, art schools

Soutien aux politiques du ministère de l.d: Central administration, HR, digital systems, and cross-cutting support for the Ministry of Culture.
Soutien aux politiques du ministère de l.b: Ministry of Culture staff and services

Presse et médias.d: Press subsidies, AFP funding, postal distribution aid, and support for media pluralism.

Livre et industries culturelles.d: Support for the book sector (BnF, public libraries), music, cinema, and cultural industries.
Livre et industries culturelles.b: BnF, public libraries, publishers, cultural industry actors

Radio France.d: Public funding for Radio France public radio broadcasting group.

Présidence de la République.d: Operating budget of the Presidency of the French Republic.

Assemblée nationale.d: Operating budget of the National Assembly, determined by its own governing body.

Sénat.d: Operating budget of the Senate, determined by its own governing body.

Conseil constitutionnel.d: Operating budget of the Constitutional Council for constitutional review and election oversight.

Cour de justice de la République.d: Operating budget of the court that tries government ministers for crimes committed in office.
Cour de justice de la République.b: Cour de justice de la République members

La Chaîne parlementaire.d: Funding for LCP-AN and Public Sénat parliamentary television channels.

Protection des droits et libertés.d: Funding for independent authorities protecting rights: Défenseur des droits, CNIL, HATVP, CNCDH, etc.
Protection des droits et libertés.b: Défenseur des droits, CNIL, HATVP, citizens

Provision relative aux rémunérations pub.d: Budgetary provision for cross-cutting public sector pay measures not allocated to specific ministries.

Ouvriers des établissements industriels.d: Retirement and disability pensions for workers of state industrial establishments (FSPOEIE).

Pensions militaires d’invalidité.d: Disability pensions and related benefits for military personnel injured in service.

Régimes sociaux et de retraite des trans.d: State subsidies to special pension schemes for SNCF railway workers and RATP transit workers.

Amortissement de la dette covid-19.d: Repayment of debt accumulated during the COVID-19 crisis through CADES transfers.

Appels en garantie de l’État.d: Payments triggered when state-guaranteed loans or commitments default.

Fonds de soutien relatif aux prêts struc.d: Support fund helping local authorities exit toxic structured loans.
Fonds de soutien relatif aux prêts struc.b: Local authorities with toxic loan exposure

Valorisation de la recherche.b: Research institutions, tech transfer offices, startups

Accélération de la modernisation des ent.d: Support for business modernization, digital transformation, and industrial competitiveness.

Écologie.d: Green investment for decarbonization, hydrogen, circular economy, and biodiversity under France 2030.
Écologie.b: Green industry actors, energy transition projects

Remboursements et dégrèvements d’impôts.d: State-funded refunds and relief on local taxes (property tax, former residence tax).

Avances sur le montant des impositions.d: Cash advances to local authorities pending actual collection of local tax revenues.

Opérations immobilières de l’État.d: Proceeds from state property sales reinvested in public real estate operations.

Désendettement de l’État.d: Allocation of state asset sale proceeds toward reducing public debt.

Prêts du Trésor à des États étrangers.d: Concessional and commercial loans from the French Treasury to foreign governments.

Avances à l’ASP pour PAC.b: ASP, French farmers receiving CAP payments

Contribution à l’équipement des collecti.b: Municipalities and local authorities for capital projects

Prévention sécurité sanitaire et offre d.d: Health prevention, health security agencies, and healthcare supply organization.
Prévention sécurité sanitaire et offre d.b: Health agencies (ANSES, SPF), healthcare providers, general public

Reversement à la sécurité sociale des re.d: Transfer of pension reserve fund (FRR) revenues back to the social security system.

Jeux olympiques et paralympiques 2024.d: State contribution to organizing the Paris 2024 Olympic and Paralympic Games and related legacy.
Jeux olympiques et paralympiques 2024.b: COJOP Paris 2024, SOLIDEO, athletes, host cities

Concours financiers aux collectivités te.d: General grants (DGF, DGE) and equalization transfers from the state to local authorities.

Concours spécifiques et administration.d: Exceptional grants to local authorities in difficulty and administration of state-local financial relations.

Performance et résilience des bâtiments.d: Energy renovation and resilience improvements for state-owned buildings.

Transformation et fonction publiques.d: Public sector modernization, digital transformation of government services, and civil service reform.
Transformation et fonction publiques.b: Government agencies, civil servants, public service users

Conduite et pilotage de la transformatio.d: Strategic coordination, shared services, and support functions for government transformation programs.

Soutien aux prestations de l’aviation ci.d: Support functions and administration of the DGAC civil aviation authority.

Navigation aérienne.d: Air traffic control, en-route and approach navigation services operated by DSNA.

Transports aériens surveillance et certi.d: Aviation safety oversight, airline certification, and aircraft airworthiness surveillance.

Contrôle et modernisation de la circulat.d: Automated speed enforcement, road traffic modernization, and toll regulation.

Plan de relance Écologie.d: Recovery plan green investments: building renovation, clean transport, biodiversity, and circular economy.
Plan de relance Écologie.b: Households, businesses, local authorities for green projects

Plan de relance Compétitivité.d: Recovery plan economic competitiveness: digital transformation, industrial relocations, and innovation support.

Indemnisation des victimes des persécuti.d: Compensation for victims of antisemitic persecution during WWII and their descendants.
Indemnisation des victimes des persécuti.b: Holocaust survivors and descendants, spoliation victims

Interventions territoriales de l’État.d: Targeted interministerial programs for specific territories (Corsica, overseas plans, etc.).

Publications officielles et information.d: Official publications (Journal Officiel), public information services, and DILA operations.
Publications officielles et information.b: DILA, citizens accessing official legal information

Recherche et enseignement supérieur en m.d: Economic research and higher education under the economy ministry, including INSEE research.

Régimes de retraite des mines.d: State subsidy to the declining special pension scheme for retired mine workers.

Avances aux collectivités régies par l’a.d: Cash advances to overseas collectivities governed by article 74 of the Constitution (Polynesia, etc.).

Budget et comptes publics.d: Ministry overseeing public finances, tax collection and refunds, state accounting, public pensions, and sovereign debt management.
Budget et comptes publics.b: Taxpayers, pensioners, state creditors, local authorities

Économie, finances et industrie.d: Ministry responsible for economic policy, industrial strategy, competition regulation, trade, and support to businesses.

Éducation nationale.d: Ministry governing primary and secondary education, curricula, teacher recruitment, and school infrastructure across France.

Armées et anciens combattants.d: Ministry managing national defense, military operations, veterans’ affairs, and defense procurement.
Armées et anciens combattants.b: Military personnel, veterans, defense industry, war victims

Intérieur.d: Ministry responsible for internal security, police, civil protection, local administration, elections, and civil liberties.
Intérieur.b: Citizens, local authorities, police forces, prefectures

Solidarités, autonomie et égalité entre.d: Ministry handling social cohesion, disability and elderly autonomy policy, child protection, and gender equality initiatives.
Solidarités, autonomie et égalité entre.b: Disabled persons, elderly, women, vulnerable populations, caregivers

Enseignement supérieur et recherche.d: Ministry overseeing universities, grandes écoles, public research organizations, and student financial aid.
Enseignement supérieur et recherche.b: University students, researchers, higher education institutions

Logement et rénovation urbaine.d: Ministry directing housing policy, social housing construction, urban renewal programs, and energy renovation of buildings.
Logement et rénovation urbaine.b: Tenants, homeowners, social housing residents, construction sector

Travail et emploi.d: Ministry governing labor law, employment policy, vocational training, apprenticeships, and workplace dialogue.

Partenariat avec les collectivités terri.d: Ministry managing relations with local governments, decentralization reforms, and financial transfers to territorial authorities.
Partenariat avec les collectivités terri.b: Municipalities, departments, regions, elected local officials

Transition écologique, énergie, climat e.d: Ministry leading ecological transition, energy policy, climate action, biodiversity protection, and natural/industrial risk prevention.
Transition écologique, énergie, climat e.b: General public, energy sector, environmental organizations, at-risk communities

Justice.d: Ministry administering the judicial system, courts, prisons, judicial protection of youth, and access to legal aid.
Justice.b: Litigants, detainees, crime victims, legal professionals, minors

Culture.d: Ministry promoting cultural heritage, the arts, architecture, museums, archives, and support to creative industries.
Culture.b: Artists, cultural institutions, heritage sites, the general public

Services du Premier ministre.d: Central government services supporting the Prime Minister’s coordination role, including strategic planning, legal advisory, and interministerial coordination.
Services du Premier ministre.b: Government ministries, Prime Minister’s office, senior civil servants

Europe et affaires étrangères.d: Ministry conducting foreign policy, European affairs, diplomacy, consular services, and international development cooperation.
Europe et affaires étrangères.b: French nationals abroad, diplomatic corps, partner countries, international organizations

Agriculture, souveraineté alimentaire et.d: Ministry governing agricultural policy, food sovereignty, forestry management, rural development, and food safety.
Agriculture, souveraineté alimentaire et.b: Farmers, agri-food industry, foresters, rural communities, consumers

Outre-Mer.d: Ministry dedicated to policies for French overseas territories, addressing their specific economic, social, and institutional needs.
Outre-Mer.b: Overseas territory residents, local governments of DOM-TOM

Santé et accès aux soins.d: Ministry directing public health policy, disease prevention, healthcare access, and health system organization.
Santé et accès aux soins.b: Patients, healthcare professionals, public health agencies

Sports, jeunesse et vie associative.d: Ministry promoting sports practice, youth engagement, and support to the associative sector and civic service.
Sports, jeunesse et vie associative.b: Athletes, youth, sports federations, nonprofits, volunteers

Fonction publique, simplification et tra.d: Ministry managing civil service reform, administrative simplification, digital government, and public sector modernization.
Fonction publique, simplification et tra.b: Civil servants, public service users, government agencies

Remboursements et dégrèvements.d: Mission covering automatic and discretionary tax refunds, rebates, and relief granted to taxpayers across all major taxes.

Avances aux collectivités territoriales.d: Mission providing cash-flow advances to local authorities, mainly monthly installments of local tax revenue collected by the state on their behalf.

Pensions.d: Mission financing civil and military retirement pensions, war-related pensions, and disability allowances for state employees.
Pensions.b: Retired civil servants, military retirees, war invalids, surviving spouses

Gestion des finances publiques.d: Mission funding the operational activities of public finance directorates including tax administration, customs, and state accounting.
Gestion des finances publiques.b: DGFiP and DGDDI staff, taxpayers, state accounting stakeholders

Régimes sociaux et de retraite.d: Mission subsidizing special pension schemes that cannot self-finance, notably for transport workers, miners, and former state industrial workers.
Régimes sociaux et de retraite.b: Retirees of SNCF, RATP, miners, SEITA, merchant navy

Pouvoirs publics.d: Mission funding the constitutional institutions: Presidency, National Assembly, Senate, Constitutional Council, and Court of Justice.
Pouvoirs publics.b: President, parliamentarians, constitutional judges, institutional staff

Contrôle de la circulation et du station.d: Mission managing automated speed enforcement, traffic fine processing, and redistribution of fine revenue to road safety and local authorities.
Contrôle de la circulation et du station.b: Road users, local authorities, road safety agencies

Gestion du patrimoine immobilier de l’Ét.d: Mission managing the state’s real estate portfolio including maintenance, acquisition, disposal, and optimization of government buildings.
Gestion du patrimoine immobilier de l’Ét.b: State agencies, government employees, France Domaine

Crédits non répartis.d: Mission holding unallocated reserve appropriations for unforeseen expenditures and public salary provisions, distributed to other missions as needed.

Engagements financiers de l’État.d: Mission covering sovereign debt service, state guarantees called upon, and debt amortization including COVID-related borrowing.

Prêts et avances à divers services de l’.d: Mission providing treasury loans and advances to various state services, agencies, and public establishments with repayment obligations.
Prêts et avances à divers services de l’.b: State agencies, public establishments, budget annexes

Participations financières de l’État.d: Mission managing the state’s shareholder portfolio, capital operations in public enterprises, and strategic equity investments.
Participations financières de l’État.b: State-owned enterprises (EDF, SNCF, etc.), Agence des participations de l’État

Économie.d: Mission funding economic development programs, business support, competition oversight, consumer protection, and industrial policy implementation.

Aide publique au développement.d: Mission channeling France’s official development assistance through bilateral and multilateral cooperation, humanitarian aid, and development finance.
Aide publique au développement.b: Developing countries, AFD, international organizations, NGOs

Prêts à des États étrangers.d: Mission providing concessional and treasury loans to foreign governments, often tied to development objectives or trade facilitation.
Prêts à des États étrangers.b: Foreign sovereign governments, primarily in Africa and emerging economies

Prêts et avances à des particuliers ou à.d: Mission providing state loans and advances to individuals and private entities for specific policy purposes such as housing or social needs.
Prêts et avances à des particuliers ou à.b: Individuals, private organizations, social economy actors

Enseignement scolaire.d: Mission funding primary and secondary education including teacher salaries, school operations, private education contracts, and agricultural education.
Enseignement scolaire.b: Students (K-12), teachers, school support staff, private schools under contract

Défense.d: Mission financing military capabilities, equipment procurement, operational readiness, military personnel, and defense research.
Défense.b: Armed forces personnel, defense industry, DGA, military intelligence

Sécurités.d: Mission funding the national police, gendarmerie, civil security, and road safety programs to ensure internal security across French territory.
Sécurités.b: Police nationale, Gendarmerie nationale, firefighters, citizens

Administration générale et territoriale.d: Mission funding prefectures, sub-prefectures, and territorial state administration ensuring government presence and public order across departments.
Administration générale et territoriale.b: Prefectures, sub-prefectures, local populations, elected officials

Immigration, asile et intégration.d: Mission managing asylum procedures, immigration enforcement, reception of asylum seekers, and integration programs for legal immigrants.

Solidarité, insertion et égalité des cha.d: Mission funding social inclusion programs, disability allowances (AAH), child protection, gender equality policies, and anti-poverty measures.
Solidarité, insertion et égalité des cha.b: Disabled persons, low-income households, women, children in protection, elderly

Recherche et enseignement supérieur.d: Mission funding university operations, student grants, public research bodies (CNRS, INSERM, etc.), and innovation ecosystems.
Recherche et enseignement supérieur.b: Universities, researchers, students, CNRS, INSERM, INRAE, CEA

Cohésion des territoires.d: Mission funding housing aid, urban renewal, territorial planning, and digital connectivity to reduce geographic inequalities.
Cohésion des territoires.b: Social housing tenants, disadvantaged urban areas, rural communities, ANRU

Travail, emploi et administration des mi.b: Job seekers, apprentices, employers, France Travail, DREETS

Écologie, développement et mobilité dura.d: Mission financing sustainable transport infrastructure, water policy, biodiversity, energy transition, risk prevention, and meteorological services.
Écologie, développement et mobilité dura.b: Transport users, environmental agencies, ADEME, Météo-France, waterway operators

Relations avec les collectivités territo.d: Mission managing financial grants (DGE, DSIL, DETR) and equalization transfers from the state to local authorities.

Contrôle et exploitation aériens.d: Mission funding air navigation services, air traffic control operations, and aviation safety oversight via the DGAC budget annexe.
Contrôle et exploitation aériens.b: Airlines, air passengers, DGAC, air traffic controllers

Audiovisuel public.d: Mission funding public broadcasting entities including France Télévisions, Radio France, France Médias Monde, Arte France, and INA.
Audiovisuel public.b: France Télévisions, Radio France, Arte, viewers and listeners

Médias, livre et industries culturelles.d: Mission supporting press pluralism, book industry, music, cinema, and cultural industries through subsidies and regulatory frameworks.
Médias, livre et industries culturelles.b: Press outlets, publishers, booksellers, libraries, cultural industry professionals

Investir pour la France de 2030.d: Mission channeling the France 2030 investment plan funds into strategic sectors: nuclear, hydrogen, semiconductors, health, space, and deep tech.
Investir pour la France de 2030.b: Innovative startups, research labs, industrial champions, BPI France

Direction de l’action du Gouvernement.d: Mission funding interministerial coordination bodies, SGDSN, DINUM, government communication, and strategic advisory councils under the PM.
Direction de l’action du Gouvernement.b: Prime Minister’s office, SGDSN, DINUM, interministerial bodies

Conseil et contrôle de l’État.d: Mission funding the Conseil d’État, Cour des comptes, CESE, and other consultative/oversight bodies ensuring administrative and financial control.
Conseil et contrôle de l’État.b: Conseil d’État, Cour des comptes, CESE, administrative courts

Action extérieure de l’État.d: Mission funding diplomatic network operations, consular services, cultural diplomacy (Instituts français, Alliance française), and French schools abroad.
Action extérieure de l’État.b: French embassies, consulates, French nationals abroad, AEFE schools

Agriculture, alimentation, forêt et affa.d: Mission funding agricultural subsidies, food safety, forestry management (ONF), rural development, and veterinary/phytosanitary services.
Agriculture, alimentation, forêt et affa.b: Farmers, agri-food businesses, ONF, rural communities, FranceAgriMer

Outre-mer.d: Mission funding specific programs for overseas territories including economic development, housing, education, and territorial continuity.
Outre-mer.b: Overseas territory residents and local governments (Guadeloupe, Martinique, Réunion, Guyane, Mayotte, etc.)

Santé.d: Mission funding public health prevention programs, health security agencies, healthcare access initiatives, and bioethics-related activities.
Santé.b: Patients, health agencies (Santé publique France, ANSM, INCa), healthcare workers

Gestion fiscale et financière de l’État.b: DGFiP agents, taxpayers, local authorities, state accountants

Pensions civiles et militaires de retrai.d: Programme paying retirement pensions to former civil servants and military personnel, plus temporary disability allowances for active agents.
Pensions civiles et militaires de retrai.b: Retired civil servants, retired military, disabled active civil servants

Charge de la dette et trésorerie de l’Ét.d: Programme covering interest payments on government bonds (OAT, BTF), debt swaps, and state cash management costs.
Charge de la dette et trésorerie de l’Ét.b: Bondholders, financial markets, Agence France Trésor

Appels en garantie de l’État (crédits év.d: Programme covering payments triggered when state-guaranteed loans or commitments default, including export guarantees and development bank guarantees.

Opérations en capital intéressant les pa.d: Programme financing capital injections, equity acquisitions, and divestiture operations in state-owned enterprises and strategic companies.
Opérations en capital intéressant les pa.b: State-owned enterprises, APE, strategic industrial companies

Désendettement de l’État et d’établissem.d: Programme using privatization and divestiture proceeds to reduce public debt of the state and its public establishments.
Désendettement de l’État et d’établissem.b: State budget, public establishments, future taxpayers

Pensions militaires d’invalidité et des.d: Programme paying disability pensions to war-injured military personnel, war victims, and their dependents, plus miscellaneous legacy pensions.
Pensions militaires d’invalidité et des.b: War invalids, war widows/orphans, veterans, victims of terrorism

Régimes de retraite et de sécurité socia.d: Programme subsidizing legacy pension and social security schemes for miners, former SEITA tobacco workers, and merchant navy personnel.
Régimes de retraite et de sécurité socia.b: Retired miners, former SEITA employees, merchant navy retirees

Compétitivité.d: Competitiveness component of the Plan de relance funding business digitalization, industrial modernization, R&D, and skills upgrading.
Compétitivité.b: SMEs, industrial firms, research labs, training organizations, BPI France

Acces au droit et a la justice.d: Ensures access to legal aid and justice for all citizens, including legal aid (aide juridictionnelle), mediation, and victim support services.
Acces au droit et a la justice.b: Citizens seeking legal assistance, low-income litigants, crime victims

Acces et retour a l’emploi.d: Supports access to employment and return-to-work programmes, including subsidized contracts and integration pathways for jobseekers.
Acces et retour a l’emploi.b: Jobseekers, long-term unemployed, youth entering labor market

Accompagnement des mutations economiques.d: Supports economic transitions and employment development through vocational training, apprenticeship incentives, and skills adaptation programmes.
Accompagnement des mutations economiques.b: Workers in economic transition, apprentices, employers

Integration et acces a la nationalite fr.d: Finances integration programmes for immigrants and manages the naturalization process, including language courses and civic training via OFII.

Actions en faveur des familles vulnerabl.d: Supported vulnerable families through child protection measures and parenting support. Merged into programme 304.

Administration penitentiaire.d: Manages the prison system including detention facilities, prison staff, inmate rehabilitation, probation services, and prison construction programmes.

Aide a l’acces au logement.d: Finances housing allowances (APL, ALS, ALF) to help low-income households afford decent housing. Funds the FNAL (Fonds national d’aide au logement).
Aide a l’acces au logement.b: Low-income tenants, students, families receiving housing benefits

Aide economique et financiere au develop.d: Provides economic and financial development aid through bilateral loans, debt relief, and contributions to multilateral development institutions (World Bank, AfDB, ADB).

Amelioration de la qualite de l’emploi e.d: Funds labour inspection, occupational health improvement, social dialogue promotion, and enforcement of labour law.
Amelioration de la qualite de l’emploi e.b: Workers, employers, trade unions, labour inspectors

Impulsion et coordination de la politiqu.d: Coordinates territorial planning policy through the ANCT (Agence nationale de la cohesion des territoires), supporting rural revitalization and balanced regional development.
Impulsion et coordination de la politiqu.b: Rural communities, underdeveloped territories, local authorities, ANCT

Paysages, eau et biodiversite.d: Manages landscape protection, water policy, and biodiversity conservation including Natura 2000 sites, national parks, and water agency coordination.
Paysages, eau et biodiversite.b: OFB, national parks, water agencies, environmental NGOs

Appels en garantie de l’Etat (credits ev.d: Covers calls on State guarantees when guaranteed entities default, including export credit guarantees (BPI) and financial sector backstops. Credits are evaluative (non-limitative).

Charge de la dette et tresorerie de l’Et.d: Covers interest payments on government debt (OAT bonds) and treasury management costs, managed by Agence France Tresor. The largest single programme by expenditure.

Concours financiers aux collectivites te.d: Provides financial transfers to local authorities (communes, departments, regions) including the DGF (dotation globale de fonctionnement), DETR, and DSIL investment grants.

Concours specifiques et administration.d: Manages specific financial support to local authorities facing exceptional circumstances (natural disasters, fiscal difficulties) and programme administration costs.
Concours specifiques et administration.b: Local authorities in financial distress, disaster-affected communes

Conduite et soutien des politiques sanit.d: Cross-cutting support for health/social ministries. Restructured into programme 155.

Conseil economique, social et environnem.d: Funds the CESE, the constitutional consultative assembly representing civil society (unions, employers, associations) that advises on economic, social, and environmental policy.

Creation.d: Supports artistic creation across performing arts (theatre, dance, music), visual arts, and contemporary creation through subsidies to cultural institutions and artists.
Creation.b: Artists, theatres, opera houses, art centers, contemporary art institutions

Developpement des entreprises et regulat.d: Supports business development, competition regulation (DGCCRF), consumer protection, industrial policy, and the postal service universal obligation (La Poste).

Urbanisme, territoires et amelioration d.d: Manages urban planning policy, housing renovation (ANAH), construction regulation, social housing oversight, and the Action Coeur de Ville programme.
Urbanisme, territoires et amelioration d.b: Social housing tenants, homeowners renovating, ANAH, local urban planners

Egalite entre les femmes et les hommes.d: Promotes gender equality through anti-violence programmes (shelters, hotline 3919), professional equality measures, and support for women’s rights associations.
Egalite entre les femmes et les hommes.b: Women, victims of domestic violence, gender equality organizations

Enseignement prive du premier et du seco.d: Funds State-contracted private primary and secondary education, covering teacher salaries and forfait communal under the Debarre law (1959).
Enseignement prive du premier et du seco.b: Students in private schools under contract, private school teachers

Enseignement scolaire public du premier.d: Finances public primary education (ecoles maternelles and elementaires), including teacher salaries, pedagogical support, and inclusive education.
Enseignement scolaire public du premier.b: Public primary school students (ages 3-11), primary school teachers

Enseignement scolaire public du second d.d: Finances public secondary education (colleges and lycees), covering teacher salaries, curriculum delivery, and school operations. The largest programme by headcount.
Enseignement scolaire public du second d.b: Public secondary school students (ages 11-18), secondary teachers

Enseignement superieur et recherche agri.d: Funds higher education and research in agriculture, veterinary science, and food science through AgroParisTech, veterinary schools, and INRAE partnerships.
Enseignement superieur et recherche agri.b: Agricultural engineering schools, veterinary schools, agronomy students

Environnement et prospective de la polit.d: Gathers intelligence, strategic analysis, and defense foresight under DGRIS, including military intelligence (DRM, DRSD), defense R&D, and international relations of the Ministry of Armed Forces.
Environnement et prospective de la polit.b: DGRIS, DRM, DRSD, defense research laboratories, IRSEM

Epargne.d: Covers State obligations related to regulated savings products, particularly interest bonuses for Livret A, LDD, LEP, and PEL savings schemes.
Epargne.b: Holders of regulated savings accounts (Livret A, LDD, LEP)

Equipement des forces.d: Funds acquisition, development, and maintenance of major military equipment (Rafale, FREMM frigates, Scorpion, Barracuda submarines, missiles) under the DGA procurement agency.
Equipement des forces.b: DGA, defense industry (Dassault, Naval Group, MBDA, Thales), armed forces

Competitivite et durabilite de l’agricul.d: Supports agricultural competitiveness and sustainability through CAP co-financing, agricultural modernization, forestry policy (ONF), and agri-food sector investments.
Competitivite et durabilite de l’agricul.b: Farmers, agri-food businesses, ONF, forestry sector, agricultural cooperatives

Formations superieures et recherche univ.d: Funds universities and higher education institutions for teaching and research. The largest programme in the MIRES mission (EUR 15.3 billion).
Formations superieures et recherche univ.b: Universities, grandes ecoles, higher education students, university researchers

Francais a l’etranger et affaires consul.d: Manages consular services for French nationals abroad (2.5 million registered) including civil status, visa processing, emergency assistance, and AEFE school network oversight.
Francais a l’etranger et affaires consul.b: French nationals abroad, foreign visa applicants, consular staff

Soutien des ministeres sociaux.d: Provides cross-cutting administrative support (HR, IT, logistics) for the ministries of labour, health, and social affairs, including DREETS regional services.
Soutien des ministeres sociaux.b: Social ministries staff, DREETS regional directorates

Gestion fiscale et financiere de l’Etat.d: Funds the DGFiP (100,000+ agents) for tax collection, public accounting, land registry (cadastre), financial management of local authorities, and property valuation.

Handicap et dependance.d: Finances the AAH disability allowance (1.2 million beneficiaries), ESAT sheltered workshops, and support for disabled persons’ inclusion in employment and society.
Handicap et dependance.b: Disabled persons, AAH recipients, MDPH, ESAT workshops

Indemnisation des victimes des persecuti.d: Provides compensation to victims of anti-Semitic persecution and acts of barbarity during World War II, including orphans and descendants under 1999 and 2004 decrees.
Indemnisation des victimes des persecuti.b: WWII persecution victims and their orphans/descendants

Expertise, information geographique et m.d: Funds Meteo-France (national weather service), IGN (geographic/mapping institute), and CEREMA (center for expertise on risks, environment, mobility, and planning).

Securite civile.d: Manages civil protection including firefighting aircraft fleet (Canadairs, Dash), bomb disposal (DGSCGC), NRBC defense, and crisis management coordination with departmental fire services.
Securite civile.b: DGSCGC, SDIS fire services, populations at risk from disasters

Interventions territoriales de l’Etat.d: Finances specific large-scale interministerial territorial projects (PEI) requiring rapid, flexible budgetary management in designated territories (Corsica, etc.).

Cour des comptes et autres juridictions.d: Funds the Cour des comptes (national audit office) and regional/territorial audit chambers (CRC/CTC) for auditing public finances and evaluating public policies.
Cour des comptes et autres juridictions.b: Cour des comptes, CRC, Haut Conseil des finances publiques

Conseil d’Etat et autres juridictions ad.d: Funds the Conseil d’Etat (supreme administrative court), 8 administrative courts of appeal, 42 administrative tribunals, and the CNDA (asylum appeal court).
Conseil d’Etat et autres juridictions ad.b: Conseil d’Etat, administrative courts, litigants in public law matters

Reconnaissance et reparation en faveur d.d: Provides pensions and benefits for veterans, maintains memorial sites, promotes remembrance through ONACVG, and strengthens defense-society links (JDC).
Reconnaissance et reparation en faveur d.b: Veterans, war widows, ONACVG, memorial sites

Meteorologie.d: Funded Meteo-France operations separately. Merged into programme 159.

Energie, climat et apres-mines.d: Finances energy transition: MaPrimeRenov housing renovation, vehicle conversion bonuses, energy cheques for 5.6 million low-income households, and post-mining territory management.
Energie, climat et apres-mines.b: Low-income households, homeowners (MaPrimeRenov), former mining regions

Hebergement, parcours vers le logement e.d: Funds emergency shelter (200,000+ places), transitional housing, social integration pathways for homeless and vulnerable persons, and the 115 emergency hotline.
Hebergement, parcours vers le logement e.b: Homeless persons, asylum seekers in temporary housing, CHRS centers

Preparation et emploi des forces.d: Funds operational readiness, training, and deployment of the French armed forces (Army, Navy, Air & Space Force), including MCO equipment maintenance and military exercises.
Preparation et emploi des forces.b: Armee de Terre, Marine nationale, Armee de l’Air et de l’Espace

Presse et medias.d: Supports press diversity and media pluralism through subsidies to press organizations, AFP (Agence France-Presse), postal distribution aid, and journalistic innovation.
Presse et medias.b: Press publishers, AFP, online media, journalists

Prevention des risques.d: Manages technological and natural risk prevention including the Barnier fund (flood/natural disaster), industrial safety regulation (ICPE/Seveso), and contaminated site remediation.
Prevention des risques.b: Populations in risk zones, ADEME, industrial facilities, local authorities

Recherche dans les domaines de l’energie.d: Funds research in energy, sustainable development, and transport, primarily through CEA (nuclear/renewable energy), IFPEN (petroleum/new energies), and IRSN (nuclear safety).
Recherche dans les domaines de l’energie.b: CEA, IFPEN, IRSN, energy research laboratories

Recherche duale (civile et militaire).d: Funds dual-purpose civil-military research through CNES (space observation, telecommunications) and CEA (nuclear simulation), supporting technologies with both civilian and defense applications.

Recherche et enseignement superieur en m.d: Funds economic research and higher education under the economy ministry, including Institut Mines-Telecom, Ecole nationale de la statistique (ENSAE), and industrial innovation support.
Recherche et enseignement superieur en m.b: Institut Mines-Telecom, ENSAE, economic research institutes

Regimes de retraite des mines, de la SEI.d: Funds the State subsidy to closed pension schemes for former miners (CANSSM), SEITA (tobacco) workers, and other legacy special retirement regimes in demographic decline.
Regimes de retraite des mines, de la SEI.b: Retired miners, former SEITA employees, legacy pension scheme beneficiaries

Regimes de retraite et de securite socia.d: Subsidizes the ENIM (Etablissement National des Invalides de la Marine) pension and social security scheme for merchant and fishing seamen, one of France’s oldest social schemes (1673).

Regimes sociaux et de retraite des trans.d: Subsidizes the special pension regimes for SNCF rail workers (CPRPSNCF) and RATP Paris transit workers, covering structural deficits of these legacy systems.
Regimes sociaux et de retraite des trans.b: Retired SNCF workers, retired RATP workers

Remboursements et degrevements d’impots.d: Covers tax refunds and relief for national taxes (income tax, corporate tax, VAT refunds) including excess payments, tax credits (CICE, CIR), and contentious refunds.
Remboursements et degrevements d’impots.b: Taxpayers entitled to refunds or tax relief

Remboursements et degrevements d’impots.d: Covers refunds and relief for local taxes (property tax, CFE) borne by the State on behalf of local authorities, including compensation for abolished housing tax.

Prevention, securite sanitaire et offre.d: Funds public health prevention, health security agencies (ANSES, Sante publique France), INCa (cancer), and healthcare provision coordination with regional health agencies (ARS).
Prevention, securite sanitaire et offre.b: Sante publique France, ANSES, INCa, ARS, general population

Affaires maritimes, peche et aquaculture.d: Manages maritime affairs including maritime safety (CROSS), fisheries regulation (EU CFP implementation), aquaculture support, and French merchant marine registry.
Affaires maritimes, peche et aquaculture.b: Fishermen, aquaculture operators, merchant marine, CROSS centers

Securite et qualite sanitaires de l’alim.d: Ensures food safety and phytosanitary controls through DGAL veterinary inspections, food chain monitoring, animal health surveillance, and plant protection.
Securite et qualite sanitaires de l’alim.b: Consumers, food industry, farmers, DGAL veterinary services

Securite et education routieres.d: Manages road safety policy through the DSCR including driving license regulation, road safety education campaigns, and the national road safety observatory.
Securite et education routieres.b: Road users, driving schools, road safety associations

Solidarite a l’egard des pays en develop.d: Implements bilateral development cooperation managed by MEAE including humanitarian aid, civil society support (NGOs), governance, health, and education programmes in partner countries.
Solidarite a l’egard des pays en develop.b: Developing countries, French development NGOs, partner states

Soutien de la politique de la defense.d: Provides cross-cutting support for defense: military HR management (DRHMD), infrastructure (SID), IT systems (DIRISI), financial management (DAF), and military justice.
Soutien de la politique de la defense.b: Ministry of Armed Forces, military personnel, defense infrastructure

Soutien de la politique de l’education n.d: Administrative support for national education: HR management, IT systems (SI), evaluation and statistics (DEPP), international cooperation, and ministry logistics for 1.1 million staff.
Soutien de la politique de l’education n.b: Ministry of National Education staff, DEPP, school administration

Conduite et pilotage des politiques de l.d: Administrative steering for agricultural policies: HR, IT, statistics (Agreste), legal affairs, and regional agricultural services (DRAAF/DAAF).
Conduite et pilotage des politiques de l.b: Ministry of Agriculture staff, DRAAF regional directorates

Conduite et pilotage des politiques de l.d: Cross-cutting administrative support for interior ministry: HR, legal affairs, IT, real estate management, and internal audit services.
Conduite et pilotage des politiques de l.b: Ministry of Interior staff and services

Conduite et pilotage des politiques de l.d: Administrative support for ecology ministry: HR, legal services, IT systems, communication, and regional DREAL directorates coordination.
Conduite et pilotage des politiques de l.b: Ministry of Ecological Transition staff, DREAL directorates

Conduite et pilotage des politiques econ.d: Cross-cutting support for economic/financial ministries: Secretary General, HR, IT modernization, legal affairs, IGPDE training institute, and communication.
Conduite et pilotage des politiques econ.b: Ministry of Economy and Finance staff, IGPDE

Statistiques et etudes economiques.d: Funds INSEE (5,000+ agents) for producing official statistics, economic studies, the national census, CPI inflation index, and national accounts.
Statistiques et etudes economiques.b: INSEE, policy makers, researchers, general public

Tourisme.d: Tourism policy through Atout France agency. Merged into programme 134.

Soutien aux politiques du ministere de l.d: Cross-cutting support for culture ministry: HR (11,000 agents), international cultural action, digital transformation, ministry operations, and DRAC coordination.
Soutien aux politiques du ministere de l.b: Ministry of Culture staff, DRAC regional directorates

Transports aeriens.d: Civil aviation policy. Transferred to DGAC budgetary annex (612-614) and programme 203.

Vie politique, cultuelle et associative.d: Elections, political party funding, religious associations. Split into programme 232 and programme 216.

Vie de l’eleve.d: Supports student welfare: school health services, national scholarships (bourses), school social work, school climate improvement, and citizenship education (Pass culture).
Vie de l’eleve.b: Primary and secondary students, school nurses, school social workers

Vie etudiante.d: Funds student welfare in higher education: CROUS scholarships (750,000 boursiers), 175,000 university housing units, RU student restaurants, and student health services.

Surete nucleaire et radioprotection.d: Funds the ASN (Autorite de surete nucleaire) for nuclear safety regulation, radiation protection, and oversight of 58 nuclear reactors and other nuclear installations.
Surete nucleaire et radioprotection.b: ASN, nuclear facility operators, general public

Aide publique au developpement - coordin.d: Coordinated overall ODA policy. Functions absorbed into programmes 110, 209, and 384.

Prevention de la delinquance et de la ra.d: Delinquency prevention and counter-radicalization via CIPDR. Absorbed into programme 216.

Facilitation et securisation des echange.d: Funds the DGDDI (Customs, 17,000 agents) for border control, trade facilitation, customs duties collection, anti-smuggling operations, and excise tax management.

Strategies economiques.d: Funds economic strategy through DG Tresor, Banque de France oversight, and France’s contributions to international economic organizations (IMF, World Bank, OECD, G7/G20).
Strategies economiques.b: DG Tresor, Banque de France, international economic organizations

Securite et protection des libertes.d: Cybersecurity (ANSSI) and digital security. Functions merged into programme 129.

Administration territoriale.d: Territorial State administration. Restructured as programme 354 from 2020.

Protection des droits et libertes.d: Funds independent administrative authorities: CNIL (data protection), Defenseur des droits (ombudsman), ARCOM (media regulation), CNCDH, HATVP, CNCTR, and CADA.
Protection des droits et libertes.b: CNIL, Defenseur des droits, ARCOM, HATVP, citizens

Entretien des batiments de l’Etat.d: Centralized State building maintenance credits. Transferred to programme 348 and CAS 723.

Transmission des savoirs et democratisat.d: Cultural education and democratization. Renumbered as programme 361.

Contribution de l’Etat a l’assurance mal.d: State employer contribution to civil servant health insurance scheme.

Contribution de l’Etat au financement de.d: State employer pension contribution, transferred to CAS Pensions account.

Financement du numerique et des usages.d: Digital infrastructure and user services. Functions merged into programme 129 and 352.

Contribution de l’Etat a la couverture d.d: State contribution to social risk coverage for transport workers under legacy schemes.

Presence francaise a l’etranger.d: French cultural presence abroad. Merged into programmes 185 and 151.

Moyens mutualises des administrations de.d: Shared resources for deconcentrated services (real estate, vehicles). Absorbed into programme 354.

Conseil superieur de la magistrature.d: Funds the CSM, the constitutional body guaranteeing judicial independence, responsible for appointing magistrates, judicial discipline, and advising on justice matters.

Dotation du Mecanisme europeen de stabil.d: France’s EUR 20.3 billion capital contributions to the ESM eurozone bailout fund.

Conduite et pilotage de la politique de.d: Administrative steering for immigration/asylum. Functions absorbed into 303 and 104.

Augmentation de capital de la Banque eur.d: France’s capital subscriptions to the EIB for EU investment capacity.

Haut Conseil des finances publiques.d: Independent fiscal council assessing macroeconomic forecasts. Absorbed into programme 164.

Conditions de vie outre-mer (former).d: Former programme number for overseas living conditions. Renumbered as programme 123.

Plan France Tres haut debit.d: Funds the national broadband plan to provide fiber-optic FTTH internet access to 100% of French territory, bridging the digital divide in rural areas.
Plan France Tres haut debit.b: Rural and underserved areas, telecom operators, local authorities
